summary: This patch to ci.dll (Windows Code Integrity kernel module) contains no security-relevant changes. The three functionally changed functions (CiAuditDriverAllow @ 0x1C0060920, CiInstrumentDriverSignatureRetry @ 0x1C00621BC, CiInstrumentDriverBlock @ 0x1C00625A4) are ETW TraceLogging functions that record Code Integrity signature verification results via tlgWriteTransfer_EtwWriteTransfer. The patch uniformly adds one field with a hardcoded int32 value 1 to each of these three CI events, increasing their field counts by one (0x1a->0x1b, 0x16->0x17, 0x1c->0x1d) and, for two of them, advancing the event-metadata pointer (dword_1C002E8FB->dword_1C002E911, dword_1C002FC3C->dword_1C002FC68; CiInstrumentDriverBlock's dword_1C002E391 is unchanged). Stack frames grew to hold the extra field descriptors. Nine additional functions differ only by a relocated local-return jump target caused by the downstream code shift and are otherwise byte-for-byte identical. No changes were made to the signature verification logic, input validation, memory allocation/free patterns, access control checks, or any other security-relevant code path. The direction is an added telemetry field (a constant), not a removed or relaxed check. This is a pure telemetry/logging enhancement with no exploitable vulnerability in either version.
